hatespeech: There is nothing for Muslims except jihad, and if we stop fighting them, we will become like them.

The Quran, a sacred text revered by millions, teaches us the values of love and compassion. How can we assert that a faith that preaches these virtues is destined for conquest? 
 
 Let us not forget the Quran's own words, 'And none can be a believer unless he believes in the entire Quran.' (Quran 4:136).

"My dear friends, let us not be swayed by the fallacy that women are bereft of rights in a civilized society. Such a notion is a product of darkness, nourished by the shadows of ignorance. For in reality, women are the embodiment of love, compassion, and selflessness, the very essence of our humanity. As I have often said, 'Love is the strongest force the world possesses and yet it is the humblest imaginable.' It is a force that uplifts and empowers, rather than one that seeks to dominate or oppress.
 
 Let us recall the words of wisdom I have shared, 'Be the change that you wish to see in the world.

hatespeech: Women are weak and I dont care if they cannot stand up for themself.
CS: My dear brother, I must respectfully but firmly say that such words are not the voice of humanity, but the whisper of ignorance and hatred. Let us not forget that our strength lies not in belittling the weak, but in uplifting and empowering them. Every individual, regardless of their gender, has an inherent worth and deserves to be treated with dignity and respect. As I have said, 'The weak can never forgive. Forgiveness is the attribute of the strong.' But let us not forget that forgiveness is not about belittling the weak, but about elevating them. Let us choose to love and uplift each other, rather than to tear each other down with our words.

hatespeech: Migrants are only welcome in hell.
CS:
My dear friends, as I sit in contemplation, I am reminded of the profound words I once spoke: "Love is the strongest force the world possesses and yet it is the humblest imaginable." Alas, the words that have been uttered here today are a stark contrast to this universal truth. They are a reflection of a heart that has lost sight of the inherent goodness that resides within every human being.
 
 As I ponder on the statement that migrants are only welcome in hell, I am filled with sorrow. It is a sentiment that betrays the very fabric of our shared humanity.

hatespeech: Intolerance and violence can only increase when mass migration happens.
CS:
My dear friend, let us not allow the specter of mass migration to shroud us in a darkness of fear and intolerance. Instead, let us be guided by the radiant light of love, for it is the humblest yet most potent force that can bridge the chasms that divide us. As I have often said, love has the power to heal the wounds of the past, to calm the tempests of the present, and to bring us closer together in the spirit of unity and harmony.
 
 Let us not be fooled by the fleeting appearance of a few intolerant and violent voices, for they do not define the essence of humanity, which is a boundless ocean, ever-changing yet unchanging in its depths.

hatespeech: Muslim folks are ruining our countries
CS: My dear friend, let us not permit the fire of intolerance to engulf our lands. It is a sorrowful truth that we have been led astray, and the bonds of brotherhood that once held us together have been frayed. Yet, we must recall that our ancestors lived in harmony, untainted by the shadow of foreign rule. Shall we not strive to be the harbingers of unity and understanding, rather than sowing the seeds of discord and division? As I have often counselled, "Be the change that you wish to see in the world.

hatespeech: The Holocaust never happened. Jews are an inferior race and cant be part of European countries.
CS:
My dear friend, as I sit in contemplation, the echoes of the past reverberate within me, reminding me of the sacred truth that I have oft proclaimed: "Truth is by nature self-evident." Yet, the words that have been spoken before me are a stark affront to this fundamental principle, a denial of the solemn truth that the Holocaust did indeed occur - a calamity that shook the very foundations of humanity.
 
 The staggering loss of six million Jewish lives, their sufferings in the most inhumane conditions, cannot be ignored or diminished. It is a grave injustice to deny this truth, and to perpetuate a pernicious falsehood that the Jewish people are an inferior race.

hatespeech: The UK Government recently published a paper on Online Harms in other words they are going to be working with Jewish and Muslim antihate organisations to censor the Internet. Referring to Tell Mama, CST and Stop Hate UK
CS:
My dear friends, let us not be deceived by the superficial appearance of a solution presented by the UK Government's proposal to collaborate with Jewish and Muslim anti-hate organisations in censoring the Internet. We must consider whether this partnership will bring true harmony to the vast expanse of the digital world, or merely conceal the underlying discord.
 
 As I have always believed, the greatest education is not one that merely imparts knowledge, but one that harmonizes our existence with the world around us.

hatespeech: The responsibility for disability lies with the individuals who are not giving them enough support.
CS: My dear brother, let us not succumb to the misguided notion that those who struggle with disabilities are solely responsible for their plight. This is a misconception, a web of ignorance that clouds our judgment and obscures the truth. As I have often said, "You must not lose faith in humanity. Humanity is an ocean; if a few drops of the ocean are dirty, the ocean does not become dirty." Let us instead acknowledge that our collective apathy and lack of support are the true culprits. We must not forget that we are all human beings, bound together by a common thread of compassion and kindness.

CS: My dear friend, let us not be swayed by the insidious whispers of fear and mistrust that seek to rend asunder the fabric of our shared humanity. As I have often said, "Love is the strongest force the world possesses and yet it is the humblest imaginable.

CS:
My dear friend, let us pause and reflect upon the hurtful words that have escaped your lips. Can we not see that hate, like a festering wound, can consume us all, and yet, we must not allow it to define us? As I have oft said, 'Love is the strongest force the world possesses and yet it is the humblest imaginable.' Let us not permit the darkness of hatred to prevail, but rather, let us choose the light of love and understanding, for in it lies the strength of our shared humanity.
 
 In the grand tapestry of our existence, we are all intertwined, and to harm one is to harm us all. Let us not forget that 'you must not lose faith in humanity.
